prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Arrr, ye be askin' why the sky be blue, eh? Well, ye see, it be all due to the sunlight scatterin' through th' air, ye scurvy dog. The tiny particles in th' atmosphere be refractin' th' shorter wavelengths o' light, which happens to be blue. So when ye look up at th' big blue yonder, ye be seein' all that scattered blue light. 'Tis a wonder o' nature, it be. Now, if ye be lookin' for a more detailed explanation, ye best be brushin' up on yer optics and atmospheric physics, ye landlubbers. Aye, 'tis a complex matter, but I hope this be enough to satisfy yer curiosity, ye bilge rat.
